import React from 'react'; import './about.css'; import Categories from '../building/categories'; import SupporterLogos from '../components/supporter-logos'; const AboutPage = () => (

Can you help us capture information on every building in Bahrain?

How many buildings are there in Bahrain? What are their characteristics? Where are they located and how do they contribute to the city? How adaptable are they? How long will they last, and what are the environmental and socio-economic implications of demolition?

Colouring Bahrain is being designed to address these questions by crowdsourcing and visualising information on Bahrain’s buildings. We’re releasing the prototype for testing in the late summer. See the slideshow below for what it will look like.

Sign up for updates
View online exhibition

Colouring Bahrain is being designed and built by BIG IT Technology partnered with University of Bahrain and funded and directed by the Bahrain Authority for Culture and Antiquities. It will launch in 2019.

Data Categories

12 categories have been chosen in consultation with specialists working in a range of areas, from energy analysis and sustainable urban planning and design to building conservation, community planning, architecture and historical research.

Once built, our platform will allow you to:

View maps

To view the data, navigate to the ‘View Maps’ page and find the category that interests you.

Add and edit data

Find a building and add or edit data for any of the 12 core categories.

See how people are using our data

Find links to visualisations and analysis, art projects and applications relating to the evolution of Bahrain, housing, energy, planning, heritage and history—or something we haven’t imagined yet.

Download, remix and reuse

Access bulk downloads of data created through the project to use and reuse under a liberal open data license. Let us know and we’ll feature showcase projects on the Colouring Bahrain site.

Keep in touch

Receive occasional newsletters about the project as it develops. You can read previous newsletters in our newsletter archive.

powered by TinyLetter. We’ll never share your email address.
); export default AboutPage;
